violations.text: Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. PIC clarified requirements with inspector during inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

Bar refrigerator was observed in poor repair, maintaining ambient temperature of 50 degrees F. PIC called for service during inspection. Foods requiring refrigeration were disposed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

A strainer and debris were observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar, a bowl was observed in the hand washing sink in the kitchen. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Staff removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

Soap and paper towel were not available at the hand-washing sink in the bar. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

Molluscan Shellfish (mussels) located in (reach-in) did not have the proper identification tags. Molluscan Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the Molluscan Shellstock are received until the container is empty. PIC retrieved tag and placed with mussels Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwashers were 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for service, all dishes and utensils to be sanitized in 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machines) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machines were taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized; ice disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

Sauces were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025
Containers of food were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the bar ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

Utensils in the kitchen are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. Staff informed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025
Blender was observed with pooled water. Blender was brought to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

The following were observed not in good repair: -bar ice machine (chipped/missing plastic) -bar low boy (not maintaining 41 or colder, foods requiring refrigeration were disposed). New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -prep unit gaskets -bar low boy -refrigeration doors -blender bowl and base. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

A hose is connected to the faucet at the basement prep sink. This faucet is lacking a backflow prevention device. An approved backflow prevention device is required in order to prevent backflow of a solid, liquid, or gas contaminant into the water supply at each point of use at a food establishment. Staff disconnected during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

The following were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and food debris:-bar top and mats-floor near service area-floor in walk-in.The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Staff began cleaning during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. PIC registered for a class to be held 12/5-6 during inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/03/2024
Three lights were observed non-functional at the time of this inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH. PIC stated they are taking a food safety course at the end of the month. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/03/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. PIC provided during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of pink residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024
ROP fish was observed thawing in the cold drawer without being removed from its packaging or cutting open. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

The door to the outside, located in the rear of the building, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024
Three hood lights were observed not functioning at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH. New Violation. Correct By: 10/03/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. PIC provided during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024
Chopped lettuce was observed in the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the kitchen dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. All dishes and utensils to be sanitized in the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice disposed. Ice machine taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

Meat was observed thawing on the counter; fish observed thawing in a prep sink. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. All items were placed under running water. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

Working containers of spices are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

The door to the outside, located in the rear of building, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024
The cold drawers under the grill were not in good repair, not maintaining an ambient temperature of 41 degrees or colder. All foods moved to other refrigeration. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024
The dishwasher in the kitchen was not dispensing sanitizer. All dishes and utensils to be sanitized in 3-bau sink until dishwasher is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024
